This show features the debut of the very first Bargain Hunt games, where antique experts go head to compete for BH Sport Relief Cup. programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Paddy McGuinness, Alex Scott, Emma Willis, Rylan Clark-Neal, Oti Mabuse and Tom Allen host the extravaganza, raising funds for good causes both in the UK and overseas. Highlights include two teams of celebrities leaving it all on the dance floor in a bid to be crowned The Greatest Dancer for Sport Relief and the rebirth of Killing Eve as Killing Steves, for a one-off Sport Relief spoof. The Battle of the Channels Celebrity Boat Race returns, and this year it's bigger than ever as BBC, ITV, Channel 4 and Sky compete for victory. In another sporting feat, Romesh Ranganathan takes on tennis legend Andy Murray in a crazy game of crazy golf. Plus, live music from artists including Rita Ora, John Newman and Celeste(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Fundraising fun, live from Media City in Salford. Tom Allen and Maya Jama see viewers through to the early hours, joined by dance sensations AJ and Curtis Pritchard to get the nation up on their feet. YouTuber KSI performs a world exclusive of his single Wake Up Call, while rising star Celeste performs her new single Strange. Plus, a look back at last summer's All-Star Sport Relief Netball match with sports captains Oti Mabuse and Jennifer Saunders, a sketch with the cast of the Netflix smash hit Stranger Things and the final round of A Question of Sport Relief(n)

programme.desc: Animated short from DreamWorks about a fastidiously organised businessman whose perspective on life is changed for ever following an unexpected encounter with a tiny fledgling bird. Featuring the voices of Jeanine Meyers and Jon Spinogatti(n)

programme.title: Trolls (2016)
programme.desc: A pair of fuzzy-headed trolls embark on a daring rescue mission when members of their tribe are kidnapped. Animation, with the voices of Anna Kendrick and Justin Timberlake(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Alex Scott looks back at the night of entertainment and fundraising, including a very special edition of Line of Duty featuring Jason Isaacs and Lee Mack(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live on the coast, including sea lions working in packs to hunt tuna in the shallows and moray eels that venture out of the water to seek out food on dry rocks. Puffins must brave predators to bring a mouthful of food back to their young and king penguins go without food for a month while they moult. Plus, footage of the bizarre Leaping Blenny, a fish that hates the water(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor and Matt Baker visit Looe in Cornwall during Cornish Pasty Week, meeting the town's pasty makers and going head to head in a competition to create their own. Margherita also finds out more about the area's seal population and the fisherman who live and work these shores. Plus, Adam Henson has his hands full as this season's lambs start to arrive, and Tom Heap discovers if the UK's leading food standards scheme is delivering when it comes to animal welfare(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns to the waterfront beside the V&A Dundee design museum, with Fiona Bruce going aboard the Discovery to reveal the epic story of the ship and Antarctic explorer Robert Falcon Scott. Items brought in to be assessed by the experts include a self-pouring teapot, a glass helmet and a piece of tartan that has been to the Moon(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Gillian is pushed to breaking point at the farm, and Caroline finds herself at the heart of an unlikely love triangle. Ellie airs PC Cheryl's suspicions about Eddie, and a guilt-ridden Raff confides a difficult secret to Alan. Ted and Harrison go on a risky adventure, while Alan and Celia find reason to laugh together again, but sobering news awaits. Derek Jacobi and Anne Reid star as the second-time-around lovers in Sally Wainwright's comedy drama, with Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker and Timothy West(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the latest Premier League action, which included Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United and West Ham United v Wolverhampton Wanderers. Jose Mourinho was aiming to avoid another defeat against his one of his former clubs, having lost to United in the reverse fixture, while the Hammers were desperate for points in their battle to avoid relegation(n)

programme.title: The Heat Is On: Sport Relief
programme.desc: Nick Grimshaw, Frankie Bridge, Rob Rinder, Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Karim Zeroual, Samantha Womack and Louise Minchin attempt to cross the world's oldest desert in Namibia. In four days, the group must travel over 100 miles, scale the highest sand dunes on Earth and avoid deadly wildlife, all under the glare of the blistering desert sun, to finally reach the world-famous Eduard Bohlen shipwreck on Africa's skeleton coast(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves old tents, four dining chairs and a bamboo dressing screen from being thrown in the tip, hoping she and two others can make money by restoring the items(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts as mother and daughter Audrey and Candice take on the light-hearted culinary challenge, with the help of chefs Ellis Barrie and Mike Reid(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: A family of five works in a corner shop to reveal how such businesses have changed over the course of the past 100 years, aided by presenter Sara Cox and social historian Polly Russell. The first episode covers shops from the Victorian era to the First World War. The family must learn to bake their own counter goods, measure loose products like tea, flour and sugar by hand, and make deliveries with a horse and cart. They also receive a visit from Kelly Holmes, who puts them through an Edwardian workout and discover the impact of food shortages on corner shops as the war drew on(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day, with reports from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three semi-detached properties in Leicester - including a shabby chic family home, and an Edwardian property with some rather eclectic interiors - are in the spotlight(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey around the nation with a visit to Tunbridge Wells in Kent. They take a stroll down the historic Pantiles, an old Regency arcade filled with food shops - including one for very discerning dogs. They also have a cookery lesson from Rosemary Shrager and visit a hop farm(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Kevin Dunne fears the framing of Doyle has failed, while the Kielys may finally be free of Eugene Casey and Johno is jealous of Patricia's new partner(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The fourth and final heat sees four more amateur designers take up the challenge as they each attempt to transform the bedroom of a house on an unusual self-build estate in south London, with three days, £1,000 and a small team of assistants to help deliver their projects. Tom Dyckhoff examines the history of the neighbourhood, before judges Sophie Robinson and Daniel Hopwood decide which contender is to leave the competition(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo concludes his search through the National Archives, unearthing memos relating to one of the SAS's most daring raids and documents pertaining to a conspiracy to overthrow the government. He also looks at how Churchill thought the unthinkable in the wake of defeating the Nazis(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts James Lewis and Catherine Southon search for items to take off to auction at Dover Castle in Kent. James unearths a silver owl inkwell and Catherine discovers an alabaster bust. Presenter Paul Martin delves into the history of the English Channel(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Hugh Dennis, Sarah Greene, Nish Kumar and Holly Walsh test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In the orchards of East Malling, Kent, Michael discovers how the Edwardians' serious attitude towards cultivation bears fruit even now. In Folkestone, he hears how the town coped with an influx of more than 100,000 refugees fleeing the German invasion in 1914. In the High Weald, Michael heads for Tenterden Town and the light railway that opened in 1900. His final stop is the medieval Cinque Port of Rye, where he tours the home and garden of author Henry James(n)

programme.title: Extreme Wales with Richard Parks
programme.desc: The presenter explores the River Teifi, taking part in adrenaline-filled activities such as tackling rapids and travelling at speed on open water. Richard then heads out to spend time meeting those who live on the banks of the river, and finds out why these adventures are so important to the Welsh people(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef takes a culinary tour of France to discover what Britain's nearest neighbours have to teach us. He begins his trip in Dieppe, where he prepares freshly caught turbot, forages for sea herbs along the Bay of the Somme, and cooks a seafood gratin. He then heads for southern Champagne, a region that was a favourite of Renoir(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: In the last of his two-part series, cameraman, director and naturalist Colin Stafford-Johnson encounters bats emerging from a labyrinth of limestone caves to feed on nectar from giant hibiscus flowers, and tiny frogs, smaller than a fingernail, living in patches of hidden emerald forest. Meanwhile, on the white sand beaches, baby green turtles emerge from their nests to make their way to the Caribbean sea. Colin also sees how the modern world is impacting this island paradise, meeting endangered Cuban Crocodiles as well as parrots under pressure from habitat loss(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Aisling Bea, Joe Lycett and David Mitchell join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at the oceans. One of the questions touches on one publisher's suggestion for an improvement to Moby Dick. As ever, the quiz sees correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: A special, fundraising edition of A Question of Sport, with Sue Barker at the helm. Joining the panel alongside captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are Mel Giedroyc, Tom Davis, Chris Kamara and Paddy McGuinness(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Katie Razzall reviews the day's important national and international news stories(n)

programme.title: Miriam's Big Fat Adventure
programme.desc: Part two of two. Miriam Margolyes sets out to find out what is being done to combat the obesity crisis, and meets people who are going to extreme and sometimes controversial lengths to lose weight. To understand the true cost of the epidemic, Miriam meets 41-year-old Angela, whose leg was amputated two years ago. Her weight meant she needed a knee operation, which led to a sepsis infection. Stuck at home relying on carers to wash, dress and feed her, Miriam is shocked to learn how Angela's weight upended her life(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Department for Work and Pensions is meant to help disabled people get back into work. In this programme, reporter Richard Butchins discovers that the DWP has lost more employment tribunals for disability discrimination than any other employer in Britain. He investigates why the DWP has paid its own employees nearly a million pounds of public money in both tribunal pay-outs and out-of-court settlements(n)

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary following murder investigations by Essex police, from the critical first few days right through to arrest and conviction. It begins with the first of three programmes charting the inquiry into the fatal stabbing of 36-year-old Courtney Valentine-Brown in Southend-on-Sea, Essex, as a team of detectives and specialists work around the clock to catch the killers(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Radio host Mark Wright and CBBC vlogger Lauren Layfield enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: On a visit to New Zealand, Steve Backshall encounters sperm whales and the curious kea, as well as a predator that lures insects to their death with a lethal light show(n)

programme.title: The Secrets of Your Food
programme.desc: Michael Mosley and James Wong reveal the physics, chemistry and biology hidden inside various foods. In the opening episode they explore how this chemistry fuels and builds bodies, with Michael sampling most people's first meal - human breast milk, which contains important fats, carbohydrates, vitamins and minerals. Michael and James also unravel why sourdough bread is so beneficial, how the rice has become a comforting staple food, and why allowing mushrooms to 'sunbathe' may help provide a welcome calcium boost(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Pets SOS
programme.desc: Help for owners to learn how to live in harmony with their unruly pets, at a crisis centre staffed by leading behavioural experts Sarah Fisher and Chirag Patel, veterinary surgeon Johanna Headley and renowned dog trainer Craig Ogilvie. Retired couple Jo and Tony reveal how their beloved pet Archie has been terrorising them and biting so hard that it has sent Jo to A&E(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Pets SOS
programme.desc: Vet Johanna Headley and dog trainer Craig Ogilvie meet a Labrador that has a penchant for eating other animals' poo, while Essex couple Jak and Lauren ask for advice to stop the daily attacks from their unsociable pet rabbit Flo. Behavioural expert Chirag Patel travels to a pub in south London where a problem pet is putting customers off their drinks. Susan Calman narrates(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Snow
programme.desc: Undercover cameras reveal how living in the snow tests animals to their limit. In New Zealand, cameras follow a group of kea parrots, revealing how friendship is at the heart of the lives of these sociable and highly intelligent birds. In the North Pole, cameras get up close to play-fighting polar bears, while on the other side of the planet at the South Pole, footage reveals the parental devotion that emperor penguins have as they try to secure the survival of their offspring and face some of the harshest conditions on Earth. In Tasmania, the spy wallaby finds love - discovering along the way an interesting courtship technique among wombats(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the relationship between food and religion and celebrate the vast range of faith-based dishes available in Britain today. They rustle up simnel cake and brisket, as well as examining the history of the hot cross bun and dropping in on an Ethiopian Christmas feast(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall continues his wildlife expedition in the West Country, turning his attention to the curious creatures of the Jurassic Coast, and the local conservationists dedicated to their care. Resident fishermen help Hugh devise a plan to witness the odd behaviour of cuttlefish up-close, and on the shingle expanse of Chesil Beach, a team of volunteers works to ensure nesting terns are able to raise their young in safety(n)

programme.title: The £100K House: Tricks of the Trade
programme.desc: In the East Riding of Yorkshire, Chris and Claire need a solution to a problem with privacy in their quirky former Post Office home. The floor-to-ceiling shop windows mean they are overlooked by passers-by, but Robert Jamison has a radical proposal. Mark and Linda live in a narrow house which is too dark and feels too small, so Piers Taylor reveals tricks that can transform it into a brighter home(n)

programme.title: Into the Woods (2014)
programme.desc: A wicked witch agrees to lift a curse on a young couple if they will help restore her once beautiful looks. Musical fantasy, based on Stephen Sondheim's hit stage show, which entwines the fairy tale stories of Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, Jack and the Beanstalk and Rapunzel. Starring Meryl Streep, James Corden and Emily Blunt, with a cameo by Johnny Depp as the Big Bad Wolf(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Wild Ireland: The Edge of the World
programme.desc: Part one of two. Natural history cinematographer Colin Stafford-Johnson begins a journey along Ireland's Atlantic coast by exploring the ancient ruins and wildlife of the Skellig Rocks in the south west, where early Christian monks built a monastery 1,500 years ago. He then ventures to the deserted Blasket Islands to witness vast numbers of seals coming ashore to fight, mate and moult during winter. Moving inland, Colin heads to the nation's highest mountain range in search of the last surviving herd of red deer, before setting off back to the coast on the trail of humpback whales en route to Clew Bay(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: In the last of his two-part series, cameraman, director and naturalist Colin Stafford-Johnson encounters bats emerging from a labyrinth of limestone caves to feed on nectar from giant hibiscus flowers, and tiny frogs, smaller than a fingernail, living in patches of hidden emerald forest. Meanwhile, on the white sand beaches, baby green turtles emerge from their nests to make their way to the Caribbean sea. Colin also sees how the modern world is impacting this island paradise, meeting endangered Cuban Crocodiles as well as parrots under pressure from habitat loss(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the Province of Quebec, taking the Charlevoix train along the north bank of the St Lawrence River to La Malbaie. Along the way he flies over the Laurentian Mountains in a seaplane, fishes for trout and views the waterfall at Montmorency from a zipwire - and that's just the first half of this leg(n)

programme.title: Miles Davis: Birth of the Cool (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary profile of the jazz bandleader, horn player and innovator whose restless determination to break boundaries and live life on his own terms made him a star, but also made him difficult to live with. In music and in life, Davis shied away from the status quo, and when he thought his work came to represent a new convention, he changed it again. Featuring studio out-takes, archive footage and photos and Davis's own words, as well as contributions from many friends and colleagues(n)

programme.title: The Place Beyond the Pines (2012)
programme.desc: A motorcycle stunt rider is reunited with his lost love and the son he has never met. He gives up his life on the road to support them, but ends up drawn into crime, attracting the attention of a troubled, ambitious cop and setting in motion a conflict that will have unforeseen consequences for both men's children. Crime drama, starring Ryan Gosling and Bradley Cooper(n)

programme.title: The Goob (2014)
programme.desc: A teenager spends every summer helping out in his mother's cafe and working on the harvest in the nearby pumpkin fields. The latest harvest season proves a very different one, as tension brews with his mother's new boyfriend and a flirtatious relationship with a foreign worker in the fields makes him consider a different future. Drama, starring Liam Walpole and Marama Corlett(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A plane crash in Quebec leaves a pilot stranded, an injured hiker drags himself three kilometres to safety in the Australian Outback and a brother saves his sibling who has lost consciousness on a free dive in the Bahamas. Narrated by Kevin Duala(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and garden designer Danny Clarke help an urban community farm in Southampton create a sheltered seating area, with a fire-pit for marshmallow toasting(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode embarks on an alphabetical journey through dishes prepared by an array of TV chefs, beginning with recipes and ingredients linked by the letter A. Highlights include Jamie Oliver's savoury apple crumble, and Sophie Dahl's toffee apple crumble(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team marks International Women's Day on the Holnicote Estate in Somerset, where Anita Rani meets Holly Purdley from the Women in Farming group, which aims to reduce the isolation often felt by women who live and work on farms. Matt Baker finds out about as innovative river restoration project that is also home to a new colony of beavers. Plus, Hannah Jackson - aka the Red Shepherdess - looks at whether times have really changed for women in farming(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Looking at the physical and mental benefits of gardening, including a visit to a unique community growing project in Dundee combating poverty and giving escape from addiction(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt introduces highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef continues his 900-mile road trip from Venice to Istanbul, sampling that array of gastronomic delights that lie on the route. In the penultimate episode Rick arrives in the Greek Peloponnese where he samples mouth-watering dishes such as rooster stew hilopites with mizithra cheese, and a home-made moussaka he claims is the best he have ever savoured. In his Symi island kitchen, Rick rolls up his sleeves to prepare galaktoboureko, a dessert of semolina custard in filo pastry, a favourite dish in Greece(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers give a traditional pudding a new look, reveal the secrets behind the ultimate fish cake and offer their take on Teesside dish chicken parmo(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len Goodman pays a visit to Scarborough-born actor Jimi Mistry, who recalls climbing into his family's Ford Capri as a child for a holiday to Castleton in the Peak District in 1979(n)

programme.title: The Cruel Sea (1953)
programme.desc: The veteran commander of a British escort vessel guides his inexperienced crew through the dangers of combat at sea during the Second World War Atlantic campaigns. Demoralised by the failure of his last command, the captain faces a life-or-death decision in his duty to sink an enemy vessel. Nautical action adventure, starring Jack Hawkins, Donald Sinden, Stanley Baker and Denholm Elliott(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Anita Manning are at Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where they assess items including a selection of golf memorabilia and a trio of Moorcroft ceramics(n)

programme.title: Wild Ireland: The Edge of the World
programme.desc: Part two of two. Colin Stafford-Johnson heads north from Clew Bay for Donegal - golden eagle country - before reaching the island's northern tip and turning east along the coast of Northern Ireland. Along the way he observes the whooper swans that fly in every autumn to escape the Arctic winter and the elusive pine marten, an animal resident in the area since the last Ice Age. Basking sharks, bats and salmon are among the other creatures Colin comes across as he seeks out the remotest corners of Ireland's wild west(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: Billy Bragg pays the Arderns a visit as they get to grips with 1960s life. The new decade brings a dizzying number of new developments, including skiffle music, hula hoops and sliced bread. It also brought about a seismic change in shopping with the introduction of self-service. The rise of supermarkets sees the personal service of the Arderns' shop under threat, forcing them to diversify their stock. The 1960s saw Sheffield becoming home to new workers from across the Commonwealth, many of whom share their memories of the era and the importance of the local corner shop to shift workers(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team sets off on a high-altitude trek from Kathmandu, capital of Nepal, to the Forbidden City of Lo Manthang, a secretive kingdom perched high on the Tibetan plateau, on the far side of the Himalayas. On a five-day expedition through the highest mountains, deepest gorges and harshest terrain on the planet, Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris battle huge river crossings, vast mudslides and dizzying mountain passes on a trip that would test even the hardiest new 4x4s to the limit. But Paddy, Freddie and Chris aren't crossing the Himalayas in new 4x4s. They're crossing the Himalayas in a small old Peugeot, a very compact old Renault, and Nepal's first (and only) home-built car(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams head out of Copan Ruinas in Western Honduras to their next checkpoint - Panama City. The journey sees them travel across vast swathes of Central America, passing through El Salvador, Nicaragua, Costa Rica and finally Panama. Mother and son Jo and Sam are the first to set off, and having raced hard in the previous leg, they're keen to experience more this time round. Working at a hostel in El Salvador offers some respite from the race, and gives the pair the chance to help with a turtle conservation project(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A colourful hair entrepreneur offers one Dragon a new look - and all of them a new investment opportunity. A cheesecake-maker has sweet dreams of a cash boost for his franchise model, but will his pitch be the stuff of nightmares? A husband-and-wife team hope their electrical invention will make sparks fly in the Den, as rival Dragons Deborah Meaden and Sara Davies line up to try it out. Presented by Evan Davis(n)

programme.title: Trigonometry
programme.desc: Accomplished chef Gemma and her paramedic boyfriend Kieran live together in west London, above Gemma's cafe. The pair are barely making ends meet, and their differing shift patterns mean they're like ships in the night. To ease some of their financial pressure, they decide to take in enigmatic lodger Ray, a former world-class synchronised swimmer whose arrival commences a modern-day love story that will change the trio's lives - and how they view relationships - for ever. Drama, starring Gary Carr, Ariane Labed and Thalissa Teixeira(n)

programme.title: Trigonometry
programme.desc: It's the morning after their shared moment in the bathroom the night before, and Gemma, Ray and Kieran try to pretend that nothing happened. Gemma opens up the cafe as usual, but is shocked when her estranged father shows up to interrogate her about her life choices. Ray takes an underwater modelling gig with Moi, but suffers a panic attack in the tank. Exhausted from back-to-back night shifts and the previous late night, Kieran gets involved in a serious incident at work. As Gemma's world falls apart around her, Ray comes to the rescue, bringing her landlords closer together in a time of crisis(n)

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the fifth round of fixtures, featuring Wales v Scotland at Cardiff Arms Park and France v Ireland at Stadium Lille Metropole(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Andy Crane and Jenny Powell host the edition first shown on May 5, featuring performances by Edelweiss, Midnight Oil, Debbie Gibson, Bon Jovi, Roxette, Kylie Minogue, Poison, Live Report, Bangles and Chaka Khan(n)

programme.title: Queens of Soul
programme.desc: A compilation of archive footage to celebrate the critically-acclaimed female singers whose voices have come to define the soul genre, including Aretha Franklin, Roberta Flack, Gladys Knight, Randy Crawford, Angie Stone, Mary J Blige and Beyonce(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first shown on May 11, featuring performances by Swing Out Sister, Chaka Khan, Yazz, Queen, Hue and Cry, Natalie Cole, Stevie Nicks, London Boys, Kylie Minogue and Stefan Dennis.(n)

programme.title: Metal Britannia
programme.desc: Documentary tracing the genre's emergence in the late 1960s, pioneered by bands including Black Sabbath and Judas Priest, who were based in the industrialised Midlands. This classic form of metal was challenged by punk in the late 1970s, before a new wave of groups such as Iron Maiden, Def Leppard and Saxon reinvigorated the style. Featuring contributions by singers Ian Gillan, Glenn Tipton, Lemmy, Bruce Dickinson and Rob Halford, guitarist Tony Iommi and keyboard player Jon Lord. Narrated by Nigel Planer(n)

programme.title: Slipknot Unmasked: All Out Life
programme.desc: A unique insight into the career and controversies of one of the most successful and contentious heavy metal bands of all time. The film combines new interviews, backstage access and an exclusive live session from the nine-piece group, performing six career-defining tracks at the legendary Maida Vale Studios in front of an intimate audience(n)

programme.title: Guitar, Drum and Bass
programme.desc: The American guitarist recounts the story of the quest for new guitar sounds, which has driven pop music from Les Paul's first guitar to Bo Diddley's tremolo, Duane Eddy's whammy bar, Keith Richards' fuzz pedal, the Who's feedback, the Byrds' 12 string, Hendrix's wah wah pedal, Uli Roth and Van Halen's shredding, the Edge's digital delay, Ry Cooder's slide and KT Tunstall and Ed Sheeran's looper pedals. Contributors include Duane Eddy, Roger McGuinn, the Edge, Bonnie Raitt, Seasick Steve, KT Tunstall, Joe Bonamassa, Uli Jon Roth, Vernon Reid, Heart's Nancy Wilson, the Runaways' Lita Ford and producer Shel Talmy(n)

programme.title: Showbands: How Ireland Learned to Party
programme.desc: The showband was a uniquely Irish phenomenon. It was a movement that saw thousands of young people travel up and down the country in the late '50s, '60s and early '70s to the 'Dancehalls of Romance' to be entertained by the Royal Showband, the Miami Showband and Big Tom and the Mainliners, and individuals including Dickie Rock, Joe Dolan and Brendan Bowyer. Ardal O'Hanlon looks back at the phenomenon, examining what triggered the infamous era, the people involved, and its eventual end in the 1980s(n)

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Having spent over 25 years in the music industry, agent Emma Banks has worked with some of the world's most famous artists and seen first-hand the fine line between success and failure. The secret to success and stardom is an elusive formula of luck, timing talent as well as the team behind the talent - the record executives, label bosses and A&R gurus. Here, Emma offers a behind-the-scenes insight into how the industry finds talent and creates superstars. Contributors include Motown legend Martha Reeves, Blur's Alex James and Labelle's Nona Hendryx(n)

programme.title: Queens of Soul
programme.desc: A compilation of archive footage to celebrate the critically-acclaimed female singers whose voices have come to define the soul genre, including Aretha Franklin, Roberta Flack, Gladys Knight, Randy Crawford, Angie Stone, Mary J Blige and Beyonce(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the home of New Nordic cuisine - Copenhagen - where he goes foraging for rosehip and learns about the art of pickling buds, bark and flowers. He's cheered to find plenty of traditional favourites, such as the city's famous Smorrebrod open sandwiches on rye bread, delicious poached turbot with cabbage and lemon butter sauce and - of course - the ubiquitous Danish pastry. Back at home, Rick cooks fried pork belly with lovage potatoes and parsley sauce, which has been voted Denmark's favourite dish(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: The struggle for survival on the region's isolated islands, home to flightless parrots, burrowing bats, giant skinks and tree-dwelling kangaroos. The documentary reveals how the arrival of new species can upset the delicate natural balance, with disastrous consequences, and explores the human history of the region. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates(n)

programme.title: Hidden
programme.desc: Cadi is informed that a body has been found at a remote petrol station outside Blaenau Ffestiniog. Suspecting the worst, she issues a warrant for Mia's arrest, but all efforts to trace her fail. Crime drama, starring Sian Reese-Williams. In English and Welsh(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Bombay
programme.desc: Clive visits the bustling Indian city, now known as Mumbai. Pursued by beggars, he makes his escape to Bollywood, where he finds stunt work in a costume epic, and also watches a street dentist and visits a roadside astrologer(n)

programme.title: Lost Sitcoms
programme.desc: A remake of the episode A Woman's Place is in the Home, originally broadcast in 1967. Alf arrives home to find a burnt supper is the least of his worries. Simon Day stars(n)

programme.title: Saturday Night Fever - The Ultimate Disco Movie
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the 40th anniversary of the blockbuster dance movie. Bruno Tonioli, a young dancer in New York in 1977, revisit the streets where the film was shot and looks back at the success of a production that gave everyone 'disco fever'. Tonioli and contributors including its star John Travolta, recount how a small-budget film and a quickly assembled soundtrack became a mega-hit that changed popular culture for ever. The programme also hears about the potent influence of impresario Robert Stigwood, whose faith in the young Travolta and then-stuttering pop group the Bee Gees proved visionary(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Andy Crane and Jenny Powell host the edition first shown on May 5, featuring performances by Edelweiss, Midnight Oil, Debbie Gibson, Bon Jovi, Roxette, Kylie Minogue, Poison, Live Report, Bangles and Chaka Khan(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first shown on May 11, featuring performances by Swing Out Sister, Chaka Khan, Yazz, Queen, Hue and Cry, Natalie Cole, Stevie Nicks, London Boys, Kylie Minogue and Stefan Dennis.(n)

programme.title: Slipknot Unmasked: All Out Life
programme.desc: A unique insight into the career and controversies of one of the most successful and contentious heavy metal bands of all time. The film combines new interviews, backstage access and an exclusive live session from the nine-piece group, performing six career-defining tracks at the legendary Maida Vale Studios in front of an intimate audience(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the home of New Nordic cuisine - Copenhagen - where he goes foraging for rosehip and learns about the art of pickling buds, bark and flowers. He's cheered to find plenty of traditional favourites, such as the city's famous Smorrebrod open sandwiches on rye bread, delicious poached turbot with cabbage and lemon butter sauce and - of course - the ubiquitous Danish pastry. Back at home, Rick cooks fried pork belly with lovage potatoes and parsley sauce, which has been voted Denmark's favourite dish(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The Wyoming Wetlands Society is a non-profit organisation that relocates beavers that have been damaging expensive landscaped areas and flooding yards and roads with their dam-building activities in Teton County. This documentary follows the work of Drew Reed as he traps the problem animals and takes them to drainage areas of the Gros Ventre River, where the beavers help to restore and enhance wetland habitats, providing many direct and indirect benefits to both wildlife and people. Rob Brydon narrates(n)

programme.title: Cake Bakers and Trouble Makers
programme.desc: In a programme originally shown to mark the Women's Institute's centenary year, historian Lucy Worsley goes beyond the stereotypes of jam and Jerusalem to reveal the surprisingly radical side of this British institution. She reveals that the WI's humble origins in an Anglesey garden shed in 1915 were no bar to the movement's grand ambitions to be an organisation engaged in the fight for women's rights. She explores some of the WI's most important campaigns, including its 1918 crusade for decent housing and its remarkably radical fight for equal pay in 1943(n)

programme.title: The Royal Ballet: Mayerling
programme.desc: Darcey Bussell and Ore Oduba introduce Kenneth MacMillan's dark and intense ballet Mayerling from the Royal Ballet. Based on the true story of the death of Crown Prince Rudolf and his young mistress Mary Vetsera in 1889, Steven McRae and Sarah Lamb take on these challenging roles in this dark and intense ballet. Crown Prince Rudolf of Austria-Hungary is emotionally unstable and haunted by his obsession with death. He is forced to marry Princess Stephanie, but soon afterwards his former lover Marie Larisch introduces him to a new mistress, Mary Vetsera, a young woman who shares his morbid fascination(n)

programme.title: Darcey's Ballet Heroes
programme.desc: After almost disappearing from ballet's centre stage - pushed aside by the newly arrived prima ballerinas over the last 100 years - the spotlight has seemingly shifted back to men. Here, ballet dancer-turned-Strictly Come Dancing judge Darcey Bussell undertakes a very personal journey to meet the heroes and dancers who transformed male ballet. Among those whose contributions she assesses are Arthur Mitchell, Anthony Dowell, Peter Schaufuss, Irek Mukhamedov and Carlos Acosta(n)

Highlights include two teams of celebrities leaving it all on the dance floor in a bid to be crowned The Greatest Dancer for Sport Relief and the rebirth of Killing Eve as Killing Steves, for a one-off Sport Relief spoof. The Battle of the Channels Celebrity Boat Race returns, and this year it's bigger than ever as BBC, ITV, Channel 4 and Sky compete for victory. In another sporting feat, Romesh Ranganathan takes on tennis legend Andy Murray in a crazy game of crazy golf. Plus, live music from artists including Rita Ora, John Newman and Celeste(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, which included Manchester City v Burnley at the Etihad Stadium and Watford v Leicester City at Vicarage Road. The current league champions have stolen a march on the Foxes in the race to finish second in the league this season, but both clubs would have been expecting to pick up three more points at home to the Clarets and Hornets respectively(n)

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz (1979)
programme.desc: A cunning convict with a history of making escape attempts is transferred to the notorious high-security island prison off the California coast, from which no one has ever broken out. Continually at odds with the brutal warden, he and three fellow prisoners hatch a plan to make it to freedom on an improvised raft. Thriller, based on a true story, starring Clint Eastwood, Patrick McGoohan, Roberts Blossom and Fred Ward(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, which included Manchester City v Burnley at the Etihad Stadium and Watford v Leicester City at Vicarage Road. The current league champions have stolen a march on the Foxes in the race to finish second in the league this season, but both clubs would have been expecting to pick up three more points at home to the Clarets and Hornets respectively(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at Patcham High School in Brighton(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway's walk in Cardigan Bay has something for everyone. For the first half he's at one with nature as he explores one of Britain's largest and oldest raised peat bogs. His route then takes him to a unique railway museum, filled with nostalgia and he also discovers the legend of Cantre Gwaelod at Sarn Cynfelyn where the shingle spit stretches an incredible 11kms out to sea. He finishes at the university town of Aberystwyth with its Victorian cliff-top pleasure park, complete with camera obscura. Derek also has a go at paddle-boarding - which has its challenges(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special edition in aid of Sport Relief in which the reds and blues are made up of Olympic legends Iwan Thomas and Tessa Sanderson, who face BBC sport presenters Manish Bhasin and John Watson. Eric Knowles hosts, while experts Caroline Haw and Danny Sebastian offer their advice. This show features the debut of the very first Bargain Hunt games, where antique experts go head to compete for BH Sport Relief Cup. Iwan also finds time to also pay a visit to the Change Foundation, a charity that runs the London Futures project which is supported by Sport Relief.(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Ahead of St Patrick's day, Claire McCollum is in Co Down to learn about the lesser-known patron St Brigid. There is also a trip to East Belfast, home of author CS Lewis(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Friends Ola and Rhesa from Leicester compete in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Anna Haugh. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: First Flight
programme.desc: Animated short from DreamWorks about a fastidiously organised businessman whose perspective on life is changed for ever following an unexpected encounter with a tiny fledgling bird. Featuring the voices of Jeanine Meyers and Jon Spinogatti(n)

programme.title: Trolls (2016)
programme.desc: A pair of fuzzy-headed trolls embark on a daring rescue mission when members of their tribe are kidnapped. Animation, with the voices of Anna Kendrick and Justin Timberlake(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Alex Scott looks back at the night of entertainment and fundraising, including a very special edition of Line of Duty featuring Jason Isaacs and Lee Mack(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live on the coast, including sea lions working in packs to hunt tuna in the shallows and moray eels that venture out of the water to seek out food on dry rocks. Puffins must brave predators to bring a mouthful of food back to their young and king penguins go without food for a month while they moult. Plus, footage of the bizarre Leaping Blenny, a fish that hates the water(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor and Matt Baker visit Looe in Cornwall during Cornish Pasty Week, meeting the town's pasty makers and going head to head in a competition to create their own. Margherita also finds out more about the area's seal population and the fisherman who live and work these shores. Plus, Adam Henson has his hands full as this season's lambs start to arrive, and Tom Heap discovers if the UK's leading food standards scheme is delivering when it comes to animal welfare(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns to the waterfront beside the V&A Dundee design museum, with Fiona Bruce going aboard the Discovery to reveal the epic story of the ship and Antarctic explorer Robert Falcon Scott. Items brought in to be assessed by the experts include a self-pouring teapot, a glass helmet and a piece of tartan that has been to the Moon(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Gillian is pushed to breaking point at the farm, and Caroline finds herself at the heart of an unlikely love triangle. Ellie airs PC Cheryl's suspicions about Eddie, and a guilt-ridden Raff confides a difficult secret to Alan. Ted and Harrison go on a risky adventure, while Alan and Celia find reason to laugh together again, but sobering news awaits. Derek Jacobi and Anne Reid star as the second-time-around lovers in Sally Wainwright's comedy drama, with Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker and Timothy West(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the latest Premier League action, which included Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United and West Ham United v Wolverhampton Wanderers. Jose Mourinho was aiming to avoid another defeat against his one of his former clubs, having lost to United in the reverse fixture, while the Hammers were desperate for points in their battle to avoid relegation(n)

programme.title: The Heat Is On: Sport Relief
programme.desc: Nick Grimshaw, Frankie Bridge, Rob Rinder, Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Karim Zeroual, Samantha Womack and Louise Minchin attempt to cross the world's oldest desert in Namibia. In four days, the group must travel over 100 miles, scale the highest sand dunes on Earth and avoid deadly wildlife, all under the glare of the blistering desert sun, to finally reach the world-famous Eduard Bohlen shipwreck on Africa's skeleton coast(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: In Lancashire, Michelle Ackerley learns how drone technology is helping police crack down on wildlife criminals and trace missing people. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two brothers want to improve the planning permission in place on a terrace in Borough Green in Kent, while a mother and daughter hope they don't have too much to do on their first investment in Liverpool. Plus, a brother and sister who are seasoned property developers have ambitious plans for a generous end-of-terrace in south London's Streatham. Presented by Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood lines up with some serious firepower, working as a US marshal in San Antonio, Texas. His mentor is Deputy Chris Bozeman, who's spent the last 15 years arresting dangerous and wanted men. After rigorous firearms and tactical training, Dom is briefed on new intel and joins live cases, executing arrest warrants on armed suspects around the city(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A plane crash in Quebec leaves a pilot stranded, an injured hiker drags himself three kilometres to safety in the Australian Outback and a brother saves his sibling who has lost consciousness on a free dive in the Bahamas. Narrated by Kevin Duala(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special edition in aid of Sport Relief in which the reds and blues are made up of Olympic legends Iwan Thomas and Tessa Sanderson, who face BBC sport presenters Manish Bhasin and John Watson. Eric Knowles hosts, while experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian offer their advice. This show features the debut of the very first Bargain Hunt games, where antique experts go head to compete for BH Sport Relief Cup. Iwan also finds time to also pay a visit to the Change Foundation, a charity that runs the London Futures project which is supported by Sport Relief.(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma comes under fire from the police in charge of Jimmi's case, while Al plays bad cop. Ayesha and Sid help when a patient's vasectomy leads to some shocking revelations(n)

programme.title: Five Bedrooms
programme.desc: Money worries overwhelm Liz, posing a threat to the potentially loved-up household. Drama, Starring Kat Stewart, Stephen Peacocke and Doris Younane(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in the Shropshire countryside to help a couple who have £425,000 to invest in a character home that gives them space for entertaining and their beloved jukebox. Away from the house search, Ginny visits the Royal Air Force Museum Cosford and meets the conservation team reconstructing a rare Hampden bomber which played an important role in the skies during the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades searches for three items he can rework to reveal their true potential. To help him on his quest, he enlists woodworker Ollie Allen and upholsterer Sharon O'Connor. Ollie has his hands full with a rickety old shelving unit, while Sharon needs to come up with a bright idea for a pair of garden chairs. Jay himself attempts to transform an unwanted side table(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Matched up with chef Ellis Barrie, loved-up bride-to-be Daniella is keen to rustle up some mouth-watering pasta dishes inspired by her Italian heritage. In the green pepper kitchen, budding groom Ross is paired with chef Akis Petretzikis. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Paddy McGuinness, Alex Scott, Emma Willis, Rylan Clark-Neal, Oti Mabuse and Tom Allen host the extravaganza, raising funds for good causes both in the UK and overseas. Highlights include two teams of celebrities leaving it all on the dance floor in a bid to be crowned The Greatest Dancer for Sport Relief and the rebirth of Killing Eve as Killing Steves, for a one-off Sport Relief spoof. The Battle of the Channels Celebrity Boat Race returns, and this year it's bigger than ever as BBC, ITV, Channel 4 and Sky compete for victory. In another sporting feat, Romesh Ranganathan takes on tennis legend Andy Murray in a crazy game of crazy golf. Plus, live music from artists including Rita Ora, John Newman and Celeste(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Fundraising fun, live from Media City in Salford. Tom Allen and Maya Jama see viewers through to the early hours, joined by dance sensations AJ and Curtis Pritchard to get the nation up on their feet. YouTuber KSI performs a world exclusive of his single Wake Up Call, while rising star Celeste performs her new single Strange. Plus, a look back at last summer's All-Star Sport Relief Netball match with sports captains Oti Mabuse and Jennifer Saunders, a sketch with the cast of the Netflix smash hit Stranger Things and the final round of A Question of Sport Relief(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity special edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as cricketer Graeme Swann and actress Rachel Parris take on Olympic hockey player Sam Quek and comedian Mark Watson(n)

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting the ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: National and international news, sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Brad Carter and Ryan Riley, and special guest Amanda Redman faces her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Family Favourites
programme.desc: Former Great British Bake Off winner Nadiya Hussain shows how to create the perfect dishes to suit any kind of day, and meets some of Britain's most inspiring home cooks along the way. She begins by demonstrating recipes for family days out, including chai-spiced vermicelli, cheese biscuits with tomato jam, a filling samosa pie, as well as a prawn saffron biryani. Her quest to find the best home-made pasty in Britain takes her to Cornwall, and she also visits a rapeseed-oil farm in Northampton(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests discuss the latest round of Premier League fixtures, including the Merseyside derby and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Dion Dublin and Martin Roberts follow the development progress of a tenement flat in Glasgow, a two-bedroom terrace in Birmingham and a house in Swindon(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v Scotland (Kick-off 2.15pm). Gabby Logan introduces coverage of the opening match in the final round of fixtures, which takes place at the Principality Stadium in Cardiff. Wales triumphed 18-11 at Murrayfield in 2019 on their way to a 27th title and will start as favourites, having been victorious in 11 of the last 12 meetings in this tournament. With analysis by current Wales international Jonathan Davies and Andy Nicol, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Chris Paterson and former dual-code star Jonathan Davies(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight are dancer and percussionist Bez, actress Maya Sondhi, former professional footballer Darren Bent, and florist and entrepreneur Elizabeth McKenna, who answer questions on beekeeping, Julie Walters, James Bond films and the life and works of Watson Fothergill(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Ireland edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Mick Foster, Tony Allen, Pauline McLynn, Vogue Williams, Clodagh Mckenna, Anna Haugh, Henry Kelly and Jimmy Cricket(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake(n)

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014)
programme.desc: A boy abducted by aliens grows up to be an interstellar mercenary. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A new paramedic joins Lev on a shift and soon catches Will's eye in the emergency department. Meanwhile, Rash nervously stands in for Archie at a conference for junior doctors and learns to trust his own judgement when he is confronted with a bullish patient. Jacob is troubled by Charlie's lack of engagement at work and tries to have a heart to heart with him, but the conversation it does not go well and Jacob is forced to take action(n)

With analysis by current Wales international Jonathan Davies and Andy Nicol, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Chris Paterson and former dual-code star Jonathan Davies(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight are dancer and percussionist Bez, actress Maya Sondhi, former professional footballer Darren Bent, and florist and entrepreneur Elizabeth McKenna, who answer questions on beekeeping, Julie Walters, James Bond films and the life and works of Watson Fothergill(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Ireland edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Mick Foster, Tony Allen, Pauline McLynn, Vogue Williams, Clodagh Mckenna, Anna Haugh, Henry Kelly and Jimmy Cricket(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake(n)

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014)
programme.desc: A boy abducted by aliens grows up to be an interstellar mercenary. He and a band of misfits break out of a prison space station with a plan to sell a mysterious orb to the highest bidder - but cross a warlord, who intends to turn it into a powerful weapon. Sci-fi adventure, starring Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana and Dave Bautista, with the voices of Bradley Cooper and Vin Diesel(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A new paramedic joins Lev on a shift and soon catches Will's eye in the emergency department. Meanwhile, Rash nervously stands in for Archie at a conference for junior doctors and learns to trust his own judgement when he is confronted with a bullish patient. Jacob is troubled by Charlie's lack of engagement at work and tries to have a heart to heart with him, but the conversation it does not go well and Jacob is forced to take action(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, which included Manchester City v Burnley at the Etihad Stadium and Watford v Leicester City at Vicarage Road. The current league champions have stolen a march on the Foxes in the race to finish second in the league this season, but both clubs would have been expecting to pick up three more points at home to the Clarets and Hornets respectively(n)

programme.title: Escape from Alcatraz (1979)
programme.desc: A cunning convict with a history of making escape attempts is transferred to the notorious high-security island prison off the California coast, from which no one has ever broken out. Continually at odds with the brutal warden, he and three fellow prisoners hatch a plan to make it to freedom on an improvised raft. Thriller, based on a true story, starring Clint Eastwood, Patrick McGoohan, Roberts Blossom and Fred Ward(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, which included Manchester City v Burnley at the Etihad Stadium and Watford v Leicester City at Vicarage Road. The current league champions have stolen a march on the Foxes in the race to finish second in the league this season, but both clubs would have been expecting to pick up three more points at home to the Clarets and Hornets respectively(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at Patcham High School in Brighton(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca Ellis wants a new start with partner Kylie and children in Australia, so they set out to enjoy a trial week in Brisbane. Nicki Chapman follows their fortunes(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special edition in aid of Sport Relief in which the reds and blues are made up of Olympic legends Iwan Thomas and Tessa Sanderson, who face BBC sport presenters Manish Bhasin and John Watson. Eric Knowles hosts, while experts Caroline Haw and Danny Sebastian offer their advice. This show features the debut of the very first Bargain Hunt games, where antique experts go head to compete for BH Sport Relief Cup. Iwan also finds time to also pay a visit to the Change Foundation, a charity that runs the London Futures project which is supported by Sport Relief.(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Ahead of St Patrick's day, Claire McCollum is in Co Down to learn about the lesser-known patron St Brigid. There is also a trip to East Belfast, home of author CS Lewis(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Friends Ola and Rhesa from Leicester compete in the culinary contest, teaming up with chefs Romy Gill and Anna Haugh. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: First Flight
programme.desc: Animated short from DreamWorks about a fastidiously organised businessman whose perspective on life is changed for ever following an unexpected encounter with a tiny fledgling bird. Featuring the voices of Jeanine Meyers and Jon Spinogatti(n)

programme.title: Trolls (2016)
programme.desc: A pair of fuzzy-headed trolls embark on a daring rescue mission when members of their tribe are kidnapped. Animation, with the voices of Anna Kendrick and Justin Timberlake(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2020
programme.desc: Alex Scott looks back at the night of entertainment and fundraising, including a very special edition of Line of Duty featuring Jason Isaacs and Lee Mack(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live on the coast, including sea lions working in packs to hunt tuna in the shallows and moray eels that venture out of the water to seek out food on dry rocks. Puffins must brave predators to bring a mouthful of food back to their young and king penguins go without food for a month while they moult. Plus, footage of the bizarre Leaping Blenny, a fish that hates the water(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor and Matt Baker visit Looe in Cornwall during Cornish Pasty Week, meeting the town's pasty makers and going head to head in a competition to create their own. Margherita also finds out more about the area's seal population and the fisherman who live and work these shores. Plus, Adam Henson has his hands full as this season's lambs start to arrive, and Tom Heap discovers if the UK's leading food standards scheme is delivering when it comes to animal welfare(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The programme returns to the waterfront beside the V&A Dundee design museum, with Fiona Bruce going aboard the Discovery to reveal the epic story of the ship and Antarctic explorer Robert Falcon Scott. Items brought in to be assessed by the experts include a self-pouring teapot, a glass helmet and a piece of tartan that has been to the Moon(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Gillian is pushed to breaking point at the farm, and Caroline finds herself at the heart of an unlikely love triangle. Ellie airs PC Cheryl's suspicions about Eddie, and a guilt-ridden Raff confides a difficult secret to Alan. Ted and Harrison go on a risky adventure, while Alan and Celia find reason to laugh together again, but sobering news awaits. Derek Jacobi and Anne Reid star as the second-time-around lovers in Sally Wainwright's comedy drama, with Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker and Timothy West(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the latest Premier League action, which included Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United and West Ham United v Wolverhampton Wanderers. Jose Mourinho was aiming to avoid another defeat against his one of his former clubs, having lost to United in the reverse fixture, while the Hammers were desperate for points in their battle to avoid relegation(n)

programme.title: The Heat Is On: Sport Relief
programme.desc: Nick Grimshaw, Frankie Bridge, Rob Rinder, Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Karim Zeroual, Samantha Womack and Louise Minchin attempt to cross the world's oldest desert in Namibia. In four days, the group must travel over 100 miles, scale the highest sand dunes on Earth and avoid deadly wildlife, all under the glare of the blistering desert sun, to finally reach the world-famous Eduard Bohlen shipwreck on Africa's skeleton coast(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A woman is murdered in a hedgerow but her son survives the attack, only to fall beneath the ice of a frozen pond as he makes his escape. DCI Vera Stanhope wades in and drags him out, and against all the odds he pulls through. As the investigation into the mother's killing unfolds, Vera finds a kindred spirit in the boy and comes to realise the key to solving the case lies deep in his memory. Detective drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, with Kerry Fox and Kieran O'Brien(n)

All would be well if he'd only not built the caravan out of Mrs. Wicket's garden she(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg

Their guest is chef Meriel Armitage, who prepares barbecue glazed jackfruit ribs(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team create a cleverly designed family garden that's full of fun for pre-school tots without being a primary-coloured plastic eyesore for the grownups who share it. Alan, David Domoney, Katie Rushworth and Frances Tophill also give tips on creating a mini-maze, creative sandpits and how to use plants to give an adventurous exotic flavour to play areas(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A masterpiece by landscape artist Henry Hogson causes a stir when it is auctioned for £400,000 after a fierce bidding war - and hours later, its former owner is tortured and killed. When a Hogson work is then stolen, Barnaby and Jones research the Midsomer painter with the help of art teacher Matilda Simms. They soon uncover evidence of forgery - but the killer is still at large. Guest starring David Bamber and Susannah Harker, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Goldfinger (1964)
programme.desc: James Bond is assigned to investigate a notorious German gold smuggler and discovers his quarry has ambitions much greater than first suspected. Pursuing the criminal mastermind around the world, Bond uncovers the villain's audacious scheme to destroy the global economy with a daring attack on Fort Knox. Spy adventure, starring Sean Connery, Gert Frobe and Honor Blackman(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: A special edition of the game show in which host Stephen Mulhern invites comedy pairings to take place. Inside No 9 stars Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, Danny John-Jules and Robert Llewellyn of Red Dwarf fame and Nina Wadia and Lynda Baron, who co-star in Still Open All Hours, are the famous duos taking part. As usual they try to guess the popular phrases in the hope of scoring enough points to win a cash prize for their charity of choice - and making it through to the Super Catchphrase to play for £50,000(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway

Stephen Mulhern hosts a surprise stunt at a mystery location in London, and the Men in Brown continue on their intergalactic quest to find out which celebrity has The Squits. Plus, one lucky audience member plays to Win the Ads(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the opening Knockout round, as 12 acts perform for a place in the live Semi-Final. programme.title: American Gangster (2007)
programme.desc: The story of 1970s drug kingpin Frank Lucas, whose innovative methods of smuggling heroin into the US made him one of the leading figures in the New York underworld. A detective is assigned to bring Lucas's empire down, but his investigation is undermined by police corruption. Ridley Scott's fact-based crime drama, starring Denzel Washington, Russell Crowe, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Josh Brolin(n)

programme.title: Rebound
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher hosts the quickfire quiz that tests the general knowledge of six contestants, who go head to head in a bid to walk away with a big cash prize. The players must give as many correct answers as they can in the time it takes the 'Rebound Bar' to move across the studio, with the money on offer rising accordingly(n)

The Carribean tour concludes at the famous sunset party at Shirley Heights, where he prepares one last cocktail recipe(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up creamy seaside pie - their take on fish pie - and also prepare chilli and nachos, big burrito cake filled with sweet potatoes, smoky peppers and spicy rice, and finish off with their New York-style cheesecake topped with strawberries. Their guest is Rachel Ama, who cooks her harissa hotpot - aubergine cooked in a hot and spicy sauce(n)

programme.title: Griff's Great Australian Adventure
programme.desc: Comedian, actor and travel enthusiast Griff Rhys Jones continues his journey across Australia on the Indian Pacific. He visits Adelaide, Broken Hill and finally Sydney to discover the unique and diverse definitions of what makes this great nation(n)

programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants James Jordan, Fiona Wade and Louis Walsh to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Nina Wadia, Linford Christie, Sarah Harding and Tyger Drew-Honey pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: After months away with her family, Ruby returns to the hospital for her pregnant sister to give birth. However, when a life-or-death medical crisis unfolds, she is forced to decide where her loyalty lies. Meanwhile, Greg gets a surprise family visitor. Medical drama set in southern India, starring Amrita Acharia, Neil Morrissey and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Drama about the secrets and dishonour among the upper echelons of 19th-century London society, adapted by Julian Fellowes from his novel of the same name. The year is 1836 and the Trenchard family have recently ascended to the aristocratic society of London's Belgravia - only for a 26-year-old love affair to come back to haunt them and jeopardise the happiness of many. Starring Tamsin Greig, Philip Glenister and Harriet Walter(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Hidden Caribbean: Havana to Haiti
programme.desc: Part one of two. The actress travels across two of the most enigmatic countries in the Caribbean - Cuba and Haiti - to explore, uncover and share the hidden gems they have to offer. Joanna kicks off her adventure in a Havana boxing gym with one of Cuba's rising female boxing stars. She visits the stars of the cabaret at the National Hotel that pre-dates the revolution, and entertains the cigar workers while they roll cigars in their factory. She visits the small town of Hershey, built on the success of the sugar industry, and visits the tomb of Che Guevara before heading off to Hemingway's favourite beach(n)

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp goes on a camping trip to Brownsea Island with his family, where he plans to catch and cook dinner for the whole family. After taking his grandson fishing, he heads for a cookery masterclass with head chef of the nearby Rick Stein restaurant. The next day, Harry and his son, Jamie Redknapp compete in a game of crazy golf(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Drama about the secrets and dishonour among the upper echelons of 19th-century London society, adapted by Julian Fellowes from his novel of the same name. The year is 1836 and the Trenchard family have recently ascended to the aristocratic society of London's Belgravia - only for a 26-year-old love affair to come back to haunt them and jeopardise the happiness of many. Starring Tamsin Greig, Philip Glenister and Harriet Walter(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Hidden Caribbean: Havana to Haiti
programme.desc: Part one of two. The actress travels across two of the most enigmatic countries in the Caribbean - Cuba and Haiti - to explore, uncover and share the hidden gems they have to offer. Joanna kicks off her adventure in a Havana boxing gym with one of Cuba's rising female boxing stars. She visits the stars of the cabaret at the National Hotel that pre-dates the revolution, and entertains the cigar workers while they roll cigars in their factory. She visits the small town of Hershey, built on the success of the sugar industry, and visits the tomb of Che Guevara before heading off to Hemingway's favourite beach(n)
